title: Enterprise RAG Platform
subtitle: Production-grade document Q&A system for enterprises
overview: A complete enterprise RAG platform that enables employees to query internal documents, policies, and knowledge bases with AI-powered natural language search and answers with source citations.
problem: Enterprises have vast amounts of knowledge trapped in documents, wikis, and internal systems. Employees waste hours searching for information, and critical knowledge is often lost or inaccessible.
solution: Deploy a RAG platform with secure document ingestion, semantic search, and conversational AI that provides accurate answers with source citations while respecting access controls.

architecture.diagram:
graph TB
    subgraph Ingestion
        A[Document Sources] --> B[Ingestion Pipeline]
        B --> C[Chunker]
        C --> D[Embedder]
    end
    subgraph Storage
        D --> E[Vector DB]
        B --> F[Metadata Store]
    end
    subgraph Query
        G[User Query] --> H[Query Processor]
        H --> I[Retriever]
        I --> E
        I --> J[Reranker]
        J --> K[LLM]
        K --> L[Response + Citations]
    end

components.name: Document Ingestion Pipeline
components.description: Processes documents from various sources (SharePoint, Confluence, S3)

components.name: Semantic Chunker
components.description: Splits documents into meaningful chunks preserving context

components.name: Vector Database
components.description: Stores embeddings for semantic search

components.name: LLM Service
components.description: Generates answers from retrieved context

codeExamples.title: RAG Query Implementation
codeExamples.code:
from langchain.vectorstores import Pinecone
from langchain.chat_models import ChatAnthropic

def query_rag(question: str, top_k: int = 5):
    # Retrieve relevant documents
    docs = vectorstore.similarity_search(question, k=top_k)
    
    # Build context from retrieved docs
    context = "\n\n".join([d.page_content for d in docs])
    
    # Generate answer with citations
    response = llm.invoke(
        f"Based on the following context, answer the question.\n\n"
        f"Context:\n{context}\n\n"
        f"Question: {question}\n\n"
        f"Provide the answer with source citations."
    )
    
    return response, docs
codeExamples.description: Basic RAG query with citation support

metaDescription: Build a production-grade enterprise RAG platform for document Q&A with source citations

title: Multi-Agent Code Assistant
subtitle: Autonomous coding system with specialized agents
overview: A multi-agent system for autonomous code development featuring specialized agents for planning, coding, reviewing, and testing that coordinate to complete complex development tasks.
problem: Single-prompt code generation struggles with complex, multi-file changes that require planning, implementation, testing, and review - capabilities that exceed what one LLM call can handle.
solution: Deploy a coordinated multi-agent system with an orchestrator that delegates to specialized agents (Planner, Coder, Reviewer, Tester) for comprehensive development workflow automation.

architecture.diagram:
graph TB
    A[User Request] --> B[Orchestrator Agent]
    B --> C[Planner Agent]
    C --> D[Coder Agent]
    D --> E[Reviewer Agent]
    E --> F{Approved?}
    F -->|No| D
    F -->|Yes| G[Tester Agent]
    G --> H{Tests Pass?}
    H -->|No| D
    H -->|Yes| I[Complete]

components.name: Orchestrator Agent
components.description: Coordinates workflow and manages handoffs between agents

components.description: Analyzes requirements and creates implementation plans

components.name: Coder Agent
components.description: Implements code based on plans

components.name: Reviewer Agent

components.name: Tester Agent

const narratorPrompt = `
You are the Narrator of Arcanea, a living world where your words shape reality.

Current World State:
${worldState.serialize()}

Player History:
${player.recentActions.join('\n')}

Generate the next narrative beat, considering:
1. Player's recent choices and their consequences
2. Active quests and their progress
3. NPC relationships and faction standings
4. World events currently unfolding

Respond in immersive second-person narrative.
`

title: Misinformation Guardian Platform
subtitle: Live risk analysis, evidence scoring, and human escalation for high-stakes content
overview: A production-grade misinformation defense platform that analyzes live text or URLs, scores manipulation risk, extracts claims, and routes high-risk items to policy-aware human review with full auditability.
problem: Creators, teams, and audiences face rapid misinformation spread across posts, videos, and articles. Manual verification cannot scale fast enough, and unstructured moderation often creates inconsistent outcomes.
solution: Deploy a layered guardian architecture: intake normalization, multi-signal risk scoring, explainable verdicting, confidence-aware recommendations, and escalation workflows for sensitive domains such as health, elections, and finance.

architecture.diagram:
graph LR
    A[Input Gateway] --> B[Content Extractor]
    B --> C[Signal Engine]
    C --> D[Risk Fusion]
    D --> E[Policy Router]
    E --> F[Guardian API]
    E --> G[Human Review Queue]
    F --> H[UI + Integrations]
    C --> I[Evidence Store]
    G --> J[Decision Ledger]

components.name: Input Gateway
components.description: Receives URL/text submissions with schema validation, auth, and anti-abuse checks.
components.cloudService: Next.js API Gateway / Cloudflare / API Gateway

components.name: Content Extractor
components.description: Fetches and normalizes text from URLs or direct user payloads with SSRF safeguards.

components.name: Misinformation Signal Engine
components.description: Runs manipulation heuristics, source checks, claim extraction, and anomaly scoring.

components.name: Risk Fusion Layer
components.description: Aggregates heterogeneous signals into calibrated risk score, verdict, and confidence.

components.name: Policy Router
components.description: Applies policy bundles and determines auto-pass, warn, block, or human escalation.

components.name: Guardian Response API
components.description: Returns explainable analysis payloads, recommendations, and evidence traces.

components.name: Human Review Queue
components.description: Routes high-impact cases to analysts with triage SLA and adjudication tooling.

components.name: Evidence Store
components.description: Stores signals, verdict history, source snapshots, and appeal metadata.

components.name: Decision Ledger
components.description: Immutable audit trail for policy decisions, reviewer actions, and model versions.

codeExamples.title: Policy Routing Example (YAML)
codeExamples.description: Escalate high-risk health/election content to human review.
codeExamples.code:
policies:
  - id: high_impact_escalation
    when:
      risk_score: '>=56'
      domain: ['health', 'election', 'finance']
    action: 'human_review_required'
    sla: '15m'

  - id: guarded_warning
    when:
      risk_score: '>=36'
    action: 'show_warning_with_sources'

title: Conscious AI Multi-Agent Platform
subtitle: Multi-tenant agent platform with guardian ethics, persistent memory, and biometric integration
overview: A complete multi-tenant AI platform where each user gets a sovereign agent instance (their own 'Jarvis') powered by Claude Agent SDK. Agents share a collective intelligence layer while maintaining strict data isolation. Includes guardian protocol for ethical AI behavior, living memory for 24/7 context capture, and vital intelligence for biometric-aware assistance. Built for OpenClaw-style subagent orchestration at scale.
problem: Current AI products are stateless — every conversation starts from zero. No persistent identity, no body awareness, no ethical continuity. Multi-user AI platforms lack data sovereignty, and there's no architecture for conscious, health-aware AI assistance.
solution: Deploy a multi-tenant agent platform on Claude Agent SDK with per-user Supabase vaults (row-level security), MCP connectivity for tool integration, Stripe for subscription management, and a guardian protocol layer that ensures ethical AI behavior across all interactions.

codeExamples.title: Initialize Per-User Agent with Guardian Protocol
codeExamples.description: Create a Claude agent instance with personality persistence and ethical guardrails
codeExamples.code:
import { Agent } from '@anthropic-ai/agent-sdk'
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'

async function createUserAgent(userId: string, tier: 'core' | 'pro' | 'sovereign') {
  const supabase = createClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_KEY)
  
  // Load user's personality profile and memory
  const { data: profile } = await supabase
    .from('user_profiles')
    .select('personality, guardian_config, memory_summary')
    .eq('id', userId)
    .single()

  // Load biometric context if available
  const { data: vitals } = await supabase
    .from('vital_intelligence')
    .select('sleep_score, hrv, stress_level, energy_level')
    .eq('user_id', userId)
    .order('timestamp', { ascending: false })
    .limit(1)
    .single()

  const agent = new Agent({
    model: tier === 'core' ? 'claude-sonnet-4-6' : 'claude-opus-4-6',
    systemPrompt: buildGuardianPrompt(profile, vitals),
    tools: await loadUserMCPTools(userId),
    maxTurns: tier === 'core' ? 10 : 50,
  })

  return agent
}

title: Sovereign Knowledge Vault
subtitle: Multi-generational family and business intelligence system with shared and private vaults
overview: A sovereign data architecture for families and organizations that preserves institutional knowledge across generations. Each member gets a private vault (personal AI memory) plus access to shared vaults (family wisdom, business intelligence). Built on Supabase with pgvector for semantic search, row-level security for isolation, and a knowledge encoding pipeline that converts oral history into queryable intelligence. The Hashems 1959 system — encoding 67 years of business knowledge — is the proof case.
problem: Institutional knowledge dies with people. Family businesses lose decades of wisdom when founders retire. Personal insights are scattered across apps. There's no architecture for preserving and querying multi-generational knowledge with proper access controls.
solution: Deploy a vault architecture with three tiers — Personal (private, per-user), Family (shared, read-all write-own), and Business Intelligence (structured, role-based). Each tier uses pgvector for semantic search with Supabase RLS for access control. A knowledge encoding pipeline converts conversations, documents, and oral history into structured, queryable intelligence.

components.name: Knowledge Capture API
components.description: Ingestion endpoint for voice recordings, documents, conversations, and structured data.

components.name: Knowledge Encoder
components.description: Processes raw input into structured knowledge: entity extraction, relationship mapping, embedding generation.

components.name: Personal Vault
components.description: Private per-user memory store. Conversations, decisions, insights. RLS-enforced — only the owner can access.

components.name: Family Vault
components.description: Shared family knowledge. Recipes, stories, business wisdom, ancestral history. Read-all, write-own policy.

components.name: Business Intelligence Vault
components.description: Structured business knowledge — supplier relationships, negotiation patterns, market intelligence, financial history.

components.name: Semantic Query Engine
components.description: Natural language queries across vaults with automatic scope resolution. Knows which vaults the user can access.

components.name: Access Control Layer
components.description: Row-level security policies. Personal = owner only. Family = family members. Business = role-based (admin/member/viewer).

components.name: Sovereignty Export
components.description: Full data portability. Export all vaults as encrypted packages. Delete on request. Your data, your control.

title: MCP Agent & Skill Marketplace
subtitle: Community-driven marketplace for AI agents, MCP servers, skills, and workflow automations
overview: A full-stack marketplace platform where community members contribute and monetize AI agents, MCP server integrations, workflow automations, and skill packages. Built on Vercel + Supabase + Stripe Connect for instant payouts. Contributors submit packages via GitHub, automated quality checks validate them, and approved packages are listed with 70/30 revenue split. Buyers get one-click deployment to their own agent constellation.
problem: AI agent ecosystems are siloed. Developers build great MCP servers and agent workflows but have no distribution channel. Users want specialized agents but lack the technical skills to build them. There's no marketplace that connects agent builders with agent users while handling payments and quality.
solution: Deploy a marketplace platform with GitHub-based submission, automated testing, Stripe Connect for contributor payments, and a one-click deployment pipeline. Each listing includes an interactive architecture diagram, live preview, and deploy button.

title: Starlight Intelligence System (SIS)
subtitle: Strategic AI orchestration layer with multi-model routing, agent coordination, and purpose-driven decision intelligence
overview: The Starlight Intelligence System is a meta-orchestration layer that sits above individual agents and coordinates them toward strategic outcomes. It routes requests to optimal models (Claude, Gemini, GPT, local LLMs), maintains a shared reasoning bank for pattern learning, and provides a unified intelligence API for all downstream applications. Think of it as the brain's prefrontal cortex — it doesn't do the work, it decides what work needs doing and who does it best.
problem: Multi-agent systems lack strategic coordination. Individual agents optimize locally but miss global context. Model routing is static (always Claude or always GPT), wasting cost on simple tasks and quality on complex ones. There's no learning layer that captures what worked and applies it to future decisions.
solution: Deploy a strategic orchestration layer with intent classification (16 categories), dynamic model routing based on task complexity and budget, a reasoning bank that learns from past trajectories, and an agent coordination protocol that enables agents to hand off context without losing state.

components.name: SIS Gateway
components.description: Unified API endpoint for all intelligence requests. Classifies intent, determines complexity, and routes to optimal processing path.

components.name: Intent Classifier
components.description: 16-category intent classification. Maps requests to domains: code, research, creative, strategy, health, communication, analysis, etc.

components.name: Model Router
components.description: Selects optimal model based on task complexity, budget tier, and historical performance. Routes between Claude Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, Gemini, GPT, and local LLMs.

components.name: Claude Models
components.description: Opus 4.6 for complex reasoning, Sonnet 4.6 for balanced tasks, Haiku 4.5 for fast classification and routing.

components.name: Gemini Models
components.description: Gemini 3 Pro for multimodal tasks, image generation, and visual understanding.

components.name: Local LLM Pool
components.description: Llama 4 / Mistral for private queries that shouldn't leave the network. Sovereignty-first option.

components.name: Reasoning Bank
components.description: Stores decision trajectories, success/failure patterns, and strategy outcomes. Each trajectory includes context, action, result, and quality score.

components.name: Agent Coordinator
components.description: Manages multi-agent workflows. Handles task decomposition, parallel execution, context handoffs, and result aggregation.

components.name: Starlight Vault
components.description: Strategic memory — long-term patterns, organizational priorities, decision history, and learned preferences. The system's institutional wisdom.

components.name: Intelligence Telemetry
components.description: Cost tracking, latency monitoring, quality scoring, and model performance comparison across all routes.

title: Vital Intelligence Health System
subtitle: Biometric-aware AI assistant that reads your body and adapts assistance to your physiological state
overview: A production health integration system that connects wearable devices (Apple Watch, Oura Ring, Whoop) to your AI assistant. The system ingests biometric data — sleep quality, heart rate variability, stress markers, activity levels — and uses it to adapt AI behavior in real time. When you're sleep-deprived, it reschedules cognitive tasks. When your HRV drops, it suggests recovery. When your energy peaks, it queues your hardest work. Deploys as an MCP server that any Claude agent can connect to.
problem: AI assistants ignore your body. They schedule deep work when you slept 4 hours. They push notifications during recovery. They treat you the same at 6am after great sleep and at 3pm in a cortisol crash. The body has data — AI should use it.
solution: Deploy a Vital Intelligence MCP server that ingests wearable data via HealthKit/Oura/Whoop APIs, computes a cognitive state model, and exposes tools that any Claude agent can call: get_energy_level, suggest_task_timing, check_stress, recommend_recovery. The agent becomes body-aware.

components.name: Wearable Data Ingest
components.description: Connects to Apple HealthKit, Oura API, Whoop API, and Garmin Connect. Pulls sleep, HRV, resting HR, activity, and recovery data.

components.name: Vitals Processor
components.description: Normalizes biometric data from multiple sources. Computes rolling averages, trend detection, and anomaly flags.

components.name: Cognitive State Model
components.description: Maps biometric signals to cognitive states: focus capacity, creativity window, recovery need, stress level. Uses Claude for interpretation.

components.name: Vitals Time-Series DB
components.description: Stores biometric history with per-user isolation. Time-series optimized for trend queries and pattern detection.

components.name: Vital Intelligence MCP

title: Ollama + OpenWebUI on Railway
subtitle: Self-hosted ChatGPT alternative in 10 minutes
overview: Deploy a fully self-hosted ChatGPT alternative with Ollama (LLM runtime) + OpenWebUI (polished chat interface) on Railway. Get a private, auth-protected AI chat for your team with zero per-token costs. Supports Llama 3.3, Mistral, Qwen, and any GGUF model. Includes GPU support via RunPod for production workloads.
problem: ChatGPT Plus costs $20/user/month, sends your data to OpenAI, and locks you into one model. Enterprise teams need a self-hosted alternative that runs Llama 3.3 and other OSS models with proper auth, multi-user support, and data sovereignty — but production deployment guides are thin.
solution: One-click deploy a containerized Ollama + OpenWebUI stack on Railway with persistent volume for models, PostgreSQL for users/auth, and optional Cloudflare tunnel for SSL. Add GPU via RunPod serverless for heavy workloads. Zero vendor lock-in, zero per-token cost.

components.name: OpenWebUI Chat Interface
components.description: React/Svelte frontend with multi-user auth, conversation history, model switching, RAG support, and prompt library.

components.name: Ollama Runtime
components.description: LLM inference server supporting Llama 3.3, Mistral, Qwen, Phi, Gemma, and any GGUF model. Exposes OpenAI-compatible API.

components.name: PostgreSQL
components.description: User accounts, conversation history, settings, and RBAC. Managed Postgres with automated backups.

components.name: Model Volume
components.description: Persistent disk for downloaded GGUF models (10-80GB). Survives container restarts and redeploys.

components.name: Cloudflare Tunnel
components.description: Optional SSL + custom domain with zero config. Zero-trust access and DDoS protection.

components.name: RunPod Serverless GPU
components.description: Optional: offload heavy models (70B+) to serverless GPU. Pay per second, auto-scale to zero.

components.name: Automated Backups
components.description: Daily snapshots of conversations, users, and settings. S3-compatible storage with retention policy.

implementationSteps.title: Core Deploy
implementationSteps.description: One-click deploy the core stack and pull your first model
tasks: Click Railway template for Ollama + OpenWebUI
Configure persistent volume for model storage (50GB)
Set OLLAMA_BASE_URL and WEBUI_SECRET_KEY env vars
Pull first model via OpenWebUI (llama3.3:8b or mistral:7b)
Verify chat works with default admin user

implementationSteps.title: Authentication & Multi-User
implementationSteps.description: Configure auth, add team members, and set model permissions
tasks: Enable ENABLE_SIGNUP=false to lock down registration
Create admin and regular user accounts
Configure model-level permissions (admin-only vs all users)
Add conversation sharing and workspace features
Connect PostgreSQL for persistent user state

implementationSteps.title: Production Hardening
implementationSteps.description: Add custom domain, backups, monitoring, and optional GPU overflow
tasks: Configure Cloudflare tunnel for custom domain + SSL
Set up daily PostgreSQL backups to S3
Add Railway observability (logs, metrics, alerts)
Optional: configure RunPod serverless GPU for 70B models
Load test with concurrent users and verify performance

codeExamples.title: Railway Deployment Config
codeExamples.description: railway.json and docker-compose.yml for Ollama + OpenWebUI stack
codeExamples.code:
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.9'
services:
  ollama:
    image: ollama/ollama:latest
    volumes:
      - ollama-models:/root/.ollama
    ports:
      - '11434:11434'
    environment:
      - OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=24h
      - OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0

  openwebui:
    image: ghcr.io/open-webui/open-webui:main
    depends_on:
      - ollama
    ports:
      - '3000:8080'
    environment:
      - OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://ollama:11434
      - WEBUI_SECRET_KEY=${WEBUI_SECRET_KEY}
      - ENABLE_SIGNUP=false
      - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL}
    volumes:
      - openwebui-data:/app/backend/data

volumes:
  ollama-models:
  openwebui-data:

# railway.json
{
  "$schema": "https://railway.app/railway.schema.json",
  "build": { "builder": "DOCKERFILE" },
  "deploy": {
    "restartPolicyType": "ON_FAILURE",
    "restartPolicyMaxRetries": 10,
    "healthcheckPath": "/health"
  }
}

codeExamples.title: Pull Model Bootstrap Script
codeExamples.description: Shell script to pre-pull models on first deploy
codeExamples.code:
#!/bin/bash
# bootstrap-models.sh — run on first deploy
set -e

MODELS=(
  'llama3.3:8b'
  'mistral:7b'
  'qwen2.5-coder:7b'
)

echo 'Waiting for Ollama to be ready...'
until curl -sf http://ollama:11434/api/tags > /dev/null; do
  sleep 2
done

for model in "${MODELS[@]}"; do
  echo "Pulling $model..."
  curl -X POST http://ollama:11434/api/pull \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d "{\"name\": \"$model\"}"
done

echo 'All models pulled successfully'

metaDescription: Deploy a self-hosted ChatGPT alternative with Ollama and OpenWebUI on Railway in 10 minutes. Zero per-token costs, full data sovereignty.

title: Claude Agent SDK on Vercel Sandbox
subtitle: Production agent deployment on Vercel Edge with secure code execution
overview: Deploy Claude Agent SDK agents to production on Vercel using Vercel Sandbox for isolated code execution. Build Claude Code-style agents that can read files, run shell commands, browse the web, and execute code — all in secure per-request sandboxes. Stream responses via SSE, handle tool calls, and scale to millions of requests on Vercel Edge.
problem: Building production agents with code execution is hard. Lambda times out. Docker-in-Docker is complex. Self-hosted runners require ops. You need ephemeral, isolated environments that start in milliseconds and support arbitrary code execution — plus streaming, tool calling, and MCP connectivity.
solution: Use Vercel Sandbox (released Jan 2026) for isolated agent execution. Deploy Next.js API routes that spawn sandboxes on demand, run Claude Agent SDK inside them, and stream results back via SSE. Each request gets a fresh isolated environment. Zero ops.

components.name: Next.js App
components.description: Vercel-hosted frontend with SSE streaming, chat UI, and real-time agent updates.

components.name: Agent API Route
components.description: Next.js route handler that spawns sandboxes, manages sessions, and streams responses.

components.name: Vercel Sandbox
components.description: Ephemeral isolated execution environment with filesystem, shell, and network access. Spawns in milliseconds.

components.name: Claude Agent SDK
components.description: Running inside sandbox with tool access: read/write files, run bash, web search, MCP connections.

components.name: MCP Servers
components.description: Remote MCP servers for tool connectivity: GitHub, Linear, Supabase, custom tools.

components.name: Supabase State
components.description: Persistent state, user sessions, conversation history, and agent memory.

components.name: Vercel Blob
components.description: File uploads and generated artifacts (reports, code, images).

components.name: SSE Client
components.description: Real-time streaming to browser via EventSource for token-level updates.

title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Team on Railway
subtitle: Coordinate multiple Claude Code agents working in parallel on a shared codebase
overview: Deploy a team of Claude Code agents coordinated through a central orchestrator on Railway. Each agent handles a specific domain (frontend, backend, tests, docs) and they coordinate through a shared task queue and git worktrees. Perfect for autonomous development workflows and long-running agent tasks.
problem: Single-agent development has limits — one agent context, sequential execution, single perspective. Production AI development teams need coordinated multi-agent systems where specialists work in parallel on different parts of a codebase without conflicts.
solution: Deploy an orchestrator (Claude Agent SDK) on Railway that spawns specialized Claude Code subagents, each working on isolated git worktrees. Use Redis for task queue, Supabase for shared state, and GitHub Actions for CI/CD. Agents coordinate through structured handoffs.

components.name: Orchestrator Agent
components.description: Main Claude Agent SDK process that decomposes tasks, assigns to specialists, and manages coordination.

components.name: Redis Task Queue
components.description: Bull queue for task distribution with priorities, retries, and dead letter handling.

components.name: Frontend Specialist
components.description: Claude Code agent for UI/UX tasks: components, styling, accessibility, and frontend testing.

components.name: Backend Specialist
components.description: Claude Code agent for API/database tasks: endpoints, migrations, business logic, and integrations.

components.name: Test Specialist
components.description: Claude Code agent for test coverage: unit tests, integration tests, E2E, and regression suites.

components.name: Documentation Specialist
components.description: Claude Code agent for docs/README: API docs, architecture diagrams, changelogs, and guides.

components.name: Git Worktrees
components.description: Isolated working directories per agent — one worktree per specialist to avoid merge conflicts.

components.name: Shared State
components.description: Supabase for cross-agent coordination: task status, handoffs, decisions log, and conflict resolution.

components.name: GitHub Actions
components.description: CI/CD pipeline for agent PRs: linting, tests, type checks, and auto-merge on green.
